original_title: Silent Ransom Group targets law firms with fake IT support calls
selection_reasoning: This demonstrates classic social engineering tactics combined with modern speed of attack. Law firms as targets add an interesting dimension of trusted institutions being vulnerable, and the progression from phone call to full compromise within hours shows how traditional defenses fail against human manipulation.

field_note.title: The False Herald's Gambit
field_note.subtitle: How Corrupted Messengers Breach the Most Careful Defenses
field_note.excerpt: The Silent Ransom Guild has perfected an ancient deception: arriving at the gates not as conquerors, but as helpful servants.
field_note.epigraph: The most impregnable fortress falls not to siege engines, but to the trusted voice that says 'I'm here to help.'
body_paragraphs: The houses of law - those careful keepers of secrets, contracts, and confidential counsel - have long prided themselves on discretion and security. Their vaults hold the private affairs of kingdoms and merchants alike, protected by both magical wards and procedural discipline. Yet even these bastions of careful practice have fallen prey to one of history's oldest deceptions: the false herald who arrives claiming to serve.

The Silent Ransom Guild has elevated this ancient craft to devastating precision. Their approach begins not with battering rams or lockpicking, but with a simple communication crystal - what we might call a 'telephone.' A voice emerges, professional and concerned, claiming to represent the very enchanters who maintain the firm's protective wards. 'We've detected anomalies,' the voice explains. 'Urgent maintenance required. Please grant us access so we may strengthen your defenses.'

What makes this deception so potent is its inversion of natural suspicion. When strangers approach seeking entry, we rightfully question their motives. But when those same strangers arrive claiming to protect us from other threats, our guard lowers. The law firms, focused as they are on external threats to their clients' secrets, found themselves opening their gates to wolves dressed as shepherds.

The speed of corruption reveals another bitter truth: once trust is granted, defensive walls become highways. Reports suggest complete compromise occurring within mere hours of initial contact. This is not the slow work of traditional siegecraft, but the rapid advance possible only when defenders themselves provide the maps and keys. The false heralds, once inside, move with the confidence of legitimate servants, accessing records and memories that would take months to steal through conventional means.
